channel.title: Context Engineering Daily
channel.description: Daily news and research updates in AI Context Engineering, Prompt Engineering, RAG, and LLM development

item.title: Compressed-Sensing-Guided, Inference-Aware Structured Reduction for Large Language Models
item.description: arXiv:2604.14156v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language models deliver strong generative performance but at the cost of massive parameter counts, memory use, and decoding latency. Prior work has shown that pruning and structured sparsity can preserve accuracy under substantial compression, w...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> prompt, large language model, arxiv, model, attention | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: MemGround: Long-Term Memory Evaluation Kit for Large Language Models in Gamified Scenarios
item.description: arXiv:2604.14158v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Current evaluations of long-term memory in LLMs are fundamentally static. By fixating on simple retrieval and short-context inference, they neglect the multifaceted nature of complex memory systems, such as dynamic state tracking and hierarchical reas...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> context, large language model, retrieval, memory, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: HUOZIIME: An On-Device LLM-enhanced Input Method for Deep Personalization
item.description: arXiv:2604.14159v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Mobile input method editors (IMEs) are the primary interface for text input, yet they remain constrained to manual typing and struggle to produce personalized text. While lightweight large language models (LLMs) make on-device auxiliary generation fea...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> large language model, arxiv, model, experiment, RAG | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Can Large Language Models Detect Methodological Flaws? Evidence from Gesture Recognition for UAV-Based Rescue Operation Based on Deep Learning
item.description: arXiv:2604.14161v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Reliable evaluation is essential in machine learning research, yet methodological flaws-particularly data leakage-continue to undermine the validity of reported results. In this work, we investigate whether large language models (LLMs) can act as inde...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> study, context, prompt, large language model, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Decoupling Scores and Text: The Politeness Principle in Peer Review
item.description: arXiv:2604.14162v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Authors often struggle to interpret peer review feedback, deriving false hope from polite comments or feeling confused by specific low scores. To investigate this, we construct a dataset of over 30,000 ICLR 2021-2025 submissions and compare acceptance...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> large language model, arxiv, ICL, model, experiment | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: SeaAlert: Critical Information Extraction From Maritime Distress Communications with Large Language Models
item.description: arXiv:2604.14163v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Maritime distress communications transmitted over very high frequency (VHF) radio are safety-critical voice messages used to report emergencies at sea. Under the Global Maritime Distress and Safety System (GMDSS), such messages follow standardized pro...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> large language model, arxiv, analysis, model, framework | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: How to Fine-Tune a Reasoning Model? A Teacher-Student Cooperation Framework to Synthesize Student-Consistent SFT Data
item.description: arXiv:2604.14164v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: A widely adopted strategy for model enhancement is to use synthetic data generated by a stronger model for supervised fine-tuning (SFT). However, for emerging reasoning models like Qwen3-8B, this approach often fails to improve reasoning capabilities ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> GPT, arxiv, model, experiment, framework | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: EviSearch: A Human in the Loop System for Extracting and Auditing Clinical Evidence for Systematic Reviews
item.description: arXiv:2604.14165v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We present EviSearch, a multi-agent extraction system that automates the creation of ontology-aligned clinical evidence tables directly from native trial PDFs while guaranteeing per-cell provenance for audit and human verification. EviSearch pairs a P...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> retrieval, arxiv, multimodal, model, vision | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Hierarchical Retrieval Augmented Generation for Adversarial Technique Annotation in Cyber Threat Intelligence Text
item.description: arXiv:2604.14166v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Mapping Cyber Threat Intelligence (CTI) text to MITRE ATT\&amp;CK technique IDs is a critical task for understanding adversary behaviors and automating threat defense. While recent Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) approaches have demonstrated prom...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> context, augmented, paper, retrieval, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Chinese Essay Rhetoric Recognition Using LoRA, In-context Learning and Model Ensemble
item.description: arXiv:2604.14167v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Rhetoric recognition is a critical component in automated essay scoring. By identifying rhetorical elements in student writing, AI systems can better assess linguistic and higher-order thinking skills, making it an essential task in the area of AI for...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> context, in-context, paper, large language model, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: SciFi: A Safe, Lightweight, User-Friendly, and Fully Autonomous Agentic AI Workflow for Scientific Applications
item.description: arXiv:2604.13180v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Recent advances in agentic AI have enabled increasingly autonomous workflows, but existing systems still face substantial challenges in achieving reliable deployment in real-world scientific research. In this work, we present a safe, lightweight, and ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> context, large language model, arxiv, research, RAG | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Numerical Instability and Chaos: Quantifying the Unpredictability of Large Language Models
item.description: arXiv:2604.13206v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: As Large Language Models (LLMs) are increasingly integrated into agentic workflows, their unpredictability stemming from numerical instability has emerged as a critical reliability issue. While recent studies have demonstrated the significant downstre...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> large language model, arxiv, analysis, transformer, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: WebXSkill: Skill Learning for Autonomous Web Agents
item.description: arXiv:2604.13318v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Autonomous web agents powered by large language models (LLMs) have shown promise in completing complex browser tasks, yet they still struggle with long-horizon workflows. A key bottleneck is the grounding gap in existing skill formulations: textual wo...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> context, large language model, retrieval, ICL, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Listening Alone, Understanding Together: Collaborative Context Recovery for Privacy-Aware AI
item.description: arXiv:2604.13348v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We introduce CONCORD, a privacy-aware asynchronous assistant-to-assistant (A2A) framework that leverages collaboration between proactive speech-based AI. As agents evolve from reactive to always-listening assistants, they face a core privacy risk (of ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> context, arxiv, framework, RAG | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: ReSS: Learning Reasoning Models for Tabular Data Prediction via Symbolic Scaffold
item.description: arXiv:2604.13392v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Tabular data remains prevalent in high-stakes domains such as healthcare and finance, where predictive models are expected to provide both high accuracy and faithful, human-understandable reasoning. While symbolic models offer verifiable logic, they l...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, RAG, model, experiment, framework | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Towards Scalable Lightweight GUI Agents via Multi-role Orchestration
item.description: arXiv:2604.13488v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Autonomous Graphical User Interface (GUI) agents powered by Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) enable digital automation on end-user devices. While scaling both parameters and data has yielded substantial gains, advanced methods still suffer fro...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> large language model, arxiv, multimodal, model, framework | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: The Devil Is in Gradient Entanglement: Energy-Aware Gradient Coordinator for Robust Generalized Category Discovery
item.description: arXiv:2604.14176v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Generalized Category Discovery (GCD) leverages labeled data to categorize unlabeled samples from known or unknown classes. Most previous methods jointly optimize supervised and unsupervised objectives and achieve promising results. However, inherent o...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, analysis, model, experiment, RAG | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: MixAtlas: Uncertainty-aware Data Mixture Optimization for Multimodal LLM Midtraining
item.description: arXiv:2604.14198v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Domain reweighting can improve sample efficiency and downstream generalization, but data-mixture optimization for multimodal midtraining remains largely unexplored. Current multimodal training recipes tune mixtures along a single dimension, typically ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> image, arxiv, multimodal, model, vision | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Shapley Value-Guided Adaptive Ensemble Learning for Explainable Financial Fraud Detection with U.S. Regulatory Compliance Validation
item.description: arXiv:2604.14231v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Financial crime costs U.S. institutions over $32 billion each year. Although AI tools for fraud detection have become more advanced, their use in real-world systems still faces a major obstacle: many of these models operate as black boxes that cannot ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> study, arxiv, transformer, model, paper | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Graph-Based Fraud Detection with Dual-Path Graph Filtering
item.description: arXiv:2604.14235v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Fraud detection on graph data can be viewed as a demanding task that requires distinguishing between different types of nodes. Because graph neural networks (GNNs) are naturally suited for processing information encoded in graph form through their mes...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, model, attention, experiment, paper | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: TOPCELL: Topology Optimization of Standard Cell via LLMs
item.description: arXiv:2604.14237v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Transistor topology optimization is a critical step in standard cell design, directly dictating diffusion sharing efficiency and downstream routability. However, identifying optimal topologies remains a persistent bottleneck, as conventional exhaustiv...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> zero-shot, large language model, arxiv, model, experiment | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Awakening Dormant Experts:Counterfactual Routing to Mitigate MoE Hallucinations
item.description: arXiv:2604.14246v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Sparse Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) models have achieved remarkable scalability, yet they remain vulnerable to hallucinations, particularly when processing long-tail knowledge. We identify that this fragility stems from static Top-$k$ routing: routers ten...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, analysis, RAG, model, experiment | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>

item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (Hugging Face Blog)

item.title: Context-Engineering - "Context engineering is the delicate art and science of filling the context window with just the right information for the next step." — Andrej Karpathy. A frontier, first-principles handbook inspired by Karpathy and 3Blue1Brown for moving beyond prompt engineering to the wider discipline of context design, orchestration, and optimization.
item.description: "Context engineering is the delicate art and science of filling the context window with just the right information for the next step." — Andrej Karpathy. A frontier, first-principles handbook inspired by Karpathy and 3Blue1Brown for moving beyond prompt engineering to the wider discipline of context design, orchestration, and optimization.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> context, context window, prompt, prompt engineering | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: awesome-prompt-engineering - A curated list of resources, tools, papers, and platforms for prompt engineering in large language models (LLMs) and generative AI.

item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: LightRAG - [EMNLP2025] "LightRAG: Simple and Fast Retrieval-Augmented Generation"
item.description: [EMNLP2025] "LightRAG: Simple and Fast Retrieval-Augmented Generation"<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> retrieval, augmented, RAG | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: KAG - KAG is a logical form-guided reasoning and retrieval framework based on OpenSPG engine and LLMs. It is used to build logical reasoning and factual Q&A solutions for professional domain knowledge bases. It can effectively overcome the shortcomings of the traditional RAG vector similarity calculation model.
item.description: KAG is a logical form-guided reasoning and retrieval framework based on OpenSPG engine and LLMs. It is used to build logical reasoning and factual Q&A solutions for professional domain knowledge bases. It can effectively overcome the shortcomings of the traditional RAG vector similarity calculation model.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> retrieval, knowledge base, model, RAG, framework | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: Kiln - Build, Evaluate, and Optimize AI Systems. Includes evals, RAG, agents, fine-tuning, synthetic data generation, dataset management, MCP, and more.

item.title: Optimizing Earth Observation Satellite Schedules under Unknown Operational Constraints: An Active Constraint Acquisition Approach
item.description: arXiv:2604.13283v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Earth Observation (EO) satellite scheduling (deciding which imaging tasks to perform and when) is a well-studied combinatorial optimization problem. Existing methods typically assume that the operational constraint model is fully specified in advance....<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> study, arxiv, RAG, model, framework | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Quantifying and Understanding Uncertainty in Large Reasoning Models
item.description: arXiv:2604.13395v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large Reasoning Models (LRMs) have recently demonstrated significant improvements in complex reasoning. While quantifying generation uncertainty in LRMs is crucial, traditional methods are often insufficient because they do not provide finite-sample g...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, RAG, model, experiment, framework | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: RiskWebWorld: A Realistic Interactive Benchmark for GUI Agents in E-commerce Risk Management
item.description: arXiv:2604.13531v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Graphical User Interface (GUI) agents show strong capabilities for automating web tasks, but existing interactive benchmarks primarily target benign, predictable consumer environments. Their effectiveness in high-stakes, investigative domains such as ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, arxiv, zero-shot, product | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>

item.title: Explainable Graph Neural Networks for Interbank Contagion Surveillance: A Regulatory-Aligned Framework for the U.S. Banking Sector
item.description: arXiv:2604.14232v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The Spatial-Temporal Graph Attention Network (ST-GAT) framework was created to serve as an explainable GNN-based solution for detecting bank distress early warning signs and for conducting macro-prudential surveillance of the interbank system in the U...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, ICL, analysis, model, attention | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>

item.title: cosmos-reason1 - Cosmos-Reason1 models understand the physical common sense and generate appropriate embodied decisions in natural language through long chain-of-thought reasoning processes.

item.title: Portfolio Optimization Proxies under Label Scarcity and Regime Shifts via Bayesian and Deterministic Students under Semi-Supervised Sandwich Training
item.description: arXiv:2604.14206v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: This paper proposes a machine learning assisted portfolio optimization framework designed for low data environments and regime uncertainty. We construct a teacher student learning pipeline in which a Conditional Value at Risk (CVaR) optimizer generate...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> augmented, arxiv, model, experiment, framework | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: OpenViking - OpenViking is an open-source context database designed specifically for AI Agents(such as openclaw). OpenViking unifies the management of context (memory, resources, and skills) that Agents need through a file system paradigm, enabling hierarchical context delivery and self-evolving.
item.description: OpenViking is an open-source context database designed specifically for AI Agents(such as openclaw). OpenViking unifies the management of context (memory, resources, and skills) that Agents need through a file system paradigm, enabling hierarchical context delivery and self-evolving.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> context, memory | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: Claude-Code-Everything-You-Need-to-Know - The ultimate all-in-one guide to mastering Claude Code. From setup, prompt engineering, commands, hooks, workflows, automation, and integrations, to MCP servers, tools, and the BMAD method—packed with step-by-step tutorials, real-world examples, and expert strategies to make this the global go-to repo for Claude mastery.
item.description: The ultimate all-in-one guide to mastering Claude Code. From setup, prompt engineering, commands, hooks, workflows, automation, and integrations, to MCP servers, tools, and the BMAD method—packed with step-by-step tutorials, real-world examples, and expert strategies to make this the global go-to repo for Claude mastery.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> prompt, prompt engineering, example, step-by-step, tool | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

A core requirement in these settings is the ability to both explore the problem space and exploit acquired knowledge effective...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, reasoning, model, release | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 40%</small>

item.title: Towards Verified and Targeted Explanations through Formal Methods
item.description: arXiv:2604.14209v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: As deep neural networks are deployed in safety-critical domains such as autonomous driving and medical diagnosis, stakeholders need explanations that are interpretable but also trustworthy with formal guarantees. Existing XAI methods fall short: heuri...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, arxiv, analysis, framework | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 40%</small>

item.title: Optimistic Policy Learning under Pessimistic Adversaries with Regret and Violation Guarantees
item.description: arXiv:2604.14243v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Real-world decision-making systems operate in environments where state transitions depend not only on the agent's actions, but also on \textbf{exogenous factors outside its control}--competing agents, environmental disturbances, or strategic adversari...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> study, model, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 40%</small>
